Simon’s Public NAO Notebook

Triturator Analysis

Author

Simon Grimm

Published

March 28, 2024

Setup

Code 
# Importing packages
import seaborn as sns
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import datetime as dt
import ast
import random
import math
import csv
import gspread as gs
from IPython.display import Markdown
from tabulate import tabulate
from collections import defaultdict
from IPython.display import Markdown
from tabulate import tabulate


def return_airline_trit_dict():
    gc = gs.oauth()
    sh = gc.open("Airlines_Triturators")
    worksheet = sh.get_worksheet(0)
    df = pd.DataFrame(worksheet.get_all_records())
    prettier_triturator = {
        "Swissport": "Swissport\nTriturator",
        "American Airlines": "American Airlines\nTriturator",
        "Unknown": "Unknown",
        "No Triturator Used": "No Triturator used",
    }
    df["Prettier Triturator"] = (
        df["Triturator"].map(prettier_triturator).fillna(df["Triturator"])
    )
    # Convert to dictionary
    airline_trit_dict = pd.Series(
        df["Prettier Triturator"].values, index=df["Airline"]
    ).to_dict()
    return airline_trit_dict


def return_flights():
    airline_trit_dict = return_airline_trit_dict()
    unmatched_airlines = defaultdict(int)
    with open("all_flights.tsv") as infile:
        lines = infile.readlines()
        us_flights = []
        lines = lines[1:]
        for line in lines:
            (
                origin,
                origin_code,
                date,
                terminal,
                equipment,
                flight,
                airline,
                nation,
                state,
                flight_time,
            ) = line.split("\t")
            airlines = ast.literal_eval(airline)
            prime_airline = airlines[0]
            prime_airline = prime_airline.strip()
            if nation == "Canada":
                triturator_status = "Unknown"
            elif prime_airline in airline_trit_dict:
                triturator_status = airline_trit_dict[prime_airline]
            elif (
                prime_airline == "JetBlue"
            ):  # Sometimes it's JetBlue, sometimes it's JetBlue Airways
                triturator_status = "Swissport\nTriturator"

            elif nation != "United States":
                triturator_status = "Swissport\nTriturator"
                unmatched_airlines[prime_airline] += 1

            else:
                triturator_status = "Unknown"
                unmatched_airlines[prime_airline] += 1
            us_flights.append(
                [
                    origin,
                    origin_code,
                    date,
                    terminal,
                    equipment,
                    flight,
                    prime_airline,
                    nation,
                    state,
                    flight_time,
                    triturator_status,
                ]
            )

        us_flights_df = pd.DataFrame(us_flights)

        us_flights_df.columns = [
            "Origin",
            "Origin Code",
            "Date",
            "Terminal",
            "Equipment",
            "Flight",
            "Prime Airline",
            "Nation",
            "State",
            "Flight Time",
            "Triturator Status",
        ]
        return us_flights_df


def origin_to_nation_dict():
    df = return_flights()
    df["Plotting Origin"] = np.where(
        df["Nation"] == "United States",
        df["State"],
        df["Nation"],
    )
    nation_dict = df.set_index("Plotting Origin")["Nation"].to_dict()
    return nation_dict


def time_to_float(time):
    time = time.strip()
    try:
        time_object = dt.datetime.strptime(time, "%H:%M:%S")
        hours = time_object.hour + time_object.minute / 60 + time_object.second / 3600
        return hours
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"conversion of time {time} failed: {e}")
        return 0


def get_first_date_and_last_date():
    df = return_flights()

    first_date = df["Date"].min()

    last_date = df["Date"].max()
    last_date = pd.to_datetime(last_date, format="%B %d, %Y")

    last_date = last_date.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
    return first_date, last_date


us_flights_df = return_flights()

Introduction

In Q1 we collected several pieces of information to better understand the composition of Project Runway triturator samples. One important piece was information on inbound flights to Boston, which included flight duration, airline, plane type, etc. This data was collated by a scraper created by Lenni, found here. A script that downloads the data from AWS can be found here.

We need to know where the waste on these flights ends up. We thus spent some time reaching out to a lot of different airlines and ground handling companies (see here for call notes). We’ve got information on the triturator usage for most airlines (though we have more trust in some pieces of information than in others). Our current high level overview looks the following way:

Flights across Triturators

Code 
def create_airline_table():
    gc = gs.oauth()
    sh = gc.open("Airlines_Triturators")
    worksheet = sh.get_worksheet(0)
    table = pd.DataFrame(worksheet.get_all_records())[
        [
            "Airline",
            "Triturator",
            "Ground handling",
            "Source",
        ]
    ]

    df = us_flights_df
    df["Flight Hours"] = df["Flight Time"].apply(time_to_float)
    airline_hours = df.groupby("Prime Airline")["Flight Hours"].sum().to_dict()

    table["Flight Hours"] = table["Airline"].map(airline_hours).fillna(0).astype(int)
    table = table.sort_values(by="Flight Hours", ascending=False)
    return table


table = create_airline_table()


Markdown(
    tabulate(
        table.values,
        headers=["Airline", "Triturator", "Ground Handling", "Source", "Flight Hours"],
        showindex=False,
    )
)
Airline Triturator Ground Handling Source Flight Hours

Air France American Airlines Triangle Email by Tom Knowles (VP at Triangle) 3459
Turkish Airlines Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport) 3448
Cathay Pacific Swissport Dnata Call with Michael McPherson (Dnata) 2931
Avianca American Airlines Triangle Email by Tom Knowles (VP at Triangle) 2384
SAS Scandinavian Airlines Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport) 2211
Hawaiian Airlines Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport) 2118
Copa Airlines American Airlines Triangle Email by Tom Knowles (VP at Triangle) 1973
Fly Play Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport) 1739
El Al Israel Airlines Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport) 1552
LATAM Airlines Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport) 1369
KLM Royal Dutch Airlines American Airlines Triangle Email by Tom Knowles (VP at Triangle) 1235
Allegiant Air American Airlines Triangle Email by Tom Knowles (VP at Triangle) 945
Hainan Airlines Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport) 756
Sun Country Airlines American Airlines Triangle Email by Tom Knowles (VP at Triangle) 570
BermudAir Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport) 452
Condor Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport) 291
Swiss Swissport Dnata Call with Michael McPherson (Dnata) 234
Porter Airlines Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport) 213
Qatar Executive Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport) 102
Japan Airlines Swissport Dnata Call with Michael McPherson (Dnata) 101
Code 
def return_plotting_df():
    df = us_flights_df
    df = df.groupby("Triturator Status").size().reset_index(name="Flights")
    df = df.sort_values(by="Flights", ascending=False)
    return df


def return_destination_trit():
    df = return_plotting_df()
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 3))
    colors = {
        "No Triturator used": "tab:orange",
        "Unknown": "tab:red",
        "Swissport\nTriturator": "tab:green",
        "American Airlines\nTriturator": "tab:blue",
    }

    df.plot(
        kind="barh",
        x="Triturator Status",
        y="Flights",
        ax=ax,
        legend=False,
        color=[colors[i] for i in df["Triturator Status"]],
    )

    ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
    ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
    plt.tick_params(axis="y", which="both", left=False, right=False)
    plt.ylabel("")
    plt.xlabel("Total Flights")
    plt.title("Total Flights per Triturator and Airline")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()


return_destination_trit()

Airlines

Flights per Airline

Code 
def return_plotting_df():
    df = us_flights_df
    df = (
        df.groupby(["Prime Airline", "Triturator Status"])
        .size()
        .reset_index(name="Flights")
    )
    df = df.pivot(index="Prime Airline", columns="Triturator Status", values="Flights")
    df["Total"] = df.sum(axis=1)
    df = df.sort_values(by="Total", ascending=False)
    df = df.drop("Total", axis=1)
    return df


def total_flights_airline_trit_plot():
    df = return_plotting_df()

    df = df.head(50)
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 7))
    df.plot.barh(stacked=True, ax=ax, width=0.8)

    # drop title of legend box
    ax.legend().set_title("")
    ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
    ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
    plt.tick_params(axis="y", which="both", left=False, right=False)
    plt.ylabel("")
    plt.xlabel("Total Flights")
    first_date, last_date = get_first_date_and_last_date()
    plt.title(
        f"Total Flights per Airline and Triturator (Top 50)\n{first_date} - {last_date}"
    )

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()


total_flights_airline_trit_plot()

Flight Hours per Airline

Code 
def return_plotting_df():
    df = us_flights_df

    df["Flight Hours"] = df["Flight Time"].apply(time_to_float)

    df = (
        df.groupby(["Prime Airline", "Triturator Status"])
        .agg({"Flight Hours": "sum"})
        .reset_index()
    )

    df = df.pivot(
        index="Prime Airline", columns="Triturator Status", values="Flight Hours"
    )

    df["Total"] = df.sum(axis=1)
    df = df.sort_values(by="Total", ascending=False)
    df = df.drop("Total", axis=1)

    return df


def total_flight_hours_airline_trit_plot():
    df = return_plotting_df()
    df = df.head(50)
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 7))
    df.plot.barh(stacked=True, ax=ax, width=0.8)
    # drop title of legend box
    ax.legend().set_title("")
    ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
    ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
    plt.tick_params(axis="y", which="both", left=False, right=False)
    plt.ylabel("")
    plt.xlabel("Total Flight Hours")
    first_date, last_date = get_first_date_and_last_date()
    plt.title(
        f"Total Flight Hours per Airline and Triturator (Top 50)\n{first_date} - {last_date}"
    )

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()


total_flight_hours_airline_trit_plot()

Airlines

Flights per Origin Location

Code 
def return_plotting_df():
    df = us_flights_df

    df["Flight Hours"] = df["Flight Time"].apply(time_to_float)

    df["Plotting Origin"] = np.where(
        df["Nation"] == "United States",
        df["State"],
        df["Nation"],
    )
    # group by origin and trit status and sum flight hours
    df = (
        df.groupby(["Plotting Origin", "Triturator Status"])
        .size()
        .reset_index(name="Flights")
    )
    df = df.pivot(
        index="Plotting Origin", columns="Triturator Status", values="Flights"
    )
    df["Total"] = df.sum(axis=1)
    df = df.sort_values(by="Total", ascending=False)
    df = df.drop("Total", axis=1)

    return df


def return_destination_trit_plot():
    df = return_plotting_df()
    nation_dict = origin_to_nation_dict()

    df = df.head(50)
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 7))
    df.plot.barh(stacked=True, ax=ax, width=0.8)

    for i, label in enumerate(ax.get_yticklabels()):
        label.set_size(9)
        origin = label.get_text()
        if nation_dict[origin] != "United States":
            label.set_size(9)
            label.set_weight("bold")
    ax.get_legend().set_title("")
    ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
    ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
    plt.tick_params(axis="y", which="both", left=False, right=False)
    plt.ylabel("")
    plt.xlabel("Total Flights")
    first_date, last_date = get_first_date_and_last_date()
    plt.title(
        f"Total Flights per Country/State and Triturator (Top 50)\n{first_date} - {last_date}"
    )

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig("triturator_destination_flighs.png", dpi=600)


return_destination_trit_plot()

Flight Hours per Origin Location

Code 
def return_plotting_df():
    df = us_flights_df

    df["Flight Hours"] = df["Flight Time"].apply(time_to_float)

    df["Plotting Origin"] = np.where(
        df["Nation"] == "United States",
        df["State"],
        df["Nation"],
    )

    df = (
        df.groupby(["Plotting Origin", "Triturator Status"])
        .agg({"Flight Hours": "sum"})
        .reset_index()
    )

    df = df.pivot(
        index="Plotting Origin",
        columns="Triturator Status",
        values="Flight Hours",
    )

    df["Total"] = df.sum(axis=1)
    df = df.sort_values(by="Total", ascending=False)
    df = df.drop("Total", axis=1)

    return df


def return_destination_trit_plot():
    df = return_plotting_df()
    nation_dict = origin_to_nation_dict()

    df = df.head(50)
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 7))
    df.plot.barh(stacked=True, ax=ax, width=0.8, color=sns.color_palette("tab10"))

    for i, label in enumerate(ax.get_yticklabels()):
        label.set_size(9)
        origin = label.get_text()
        if nation_dict[origin] != "United States":
            label.set_weight("bold")

    ax.get_legend().set_title("")
    ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
    ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
    plt.tick_params(axis="y", which="both", left=False, right=False)
    plt.ylabel("")
    plt.xlabel("Total Flight Hours")
    first_date, last_date = get_first_date_and_last_date()
    plt.title(
        f"Total Flight Hours per Country/State and Triturator (Top 50)\n{first_date} - {last_date}"
    )

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig("triturator_destination_flight_hours.png", dpi=600)


return_destination_trit_plot()

Remaining airlines we are unsure about

Code 
def return_plotting_df():
    df = us_flights_df

    df["Flight Hours"] = df["Flight Time"].apply(time_to_float)
    df = df[df["Triturator Status"] == "Unknown"]

    df = (
        df.groupby(["Prime Airline", "Triturator Status"])
        .agg({"Flight Hours": "sum"})
        .reset_index()
    )
    df = df[df["Flight Hours"] > 1000]

    df = df.pivot(
        index="Prime Airline", columns="Triturator Status", values="Flight Hours"
    )

    df["Total"] = df.sum(axis=1)
    df = df.sort_values(by="Total", ascending=False)
    df = df.drop("Total", axis=1)

    return df


def return_destination_trit_plot():
    df = return_plotting_df()

    df = df.head(50)
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 3))
    df.plot.barh(stacked=True, ax=ax, width=0.8)

    ax.legend().set_visible(False)
    ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
    ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
    plt.tick_params(axis="y", which="both", left=False, right=False)
    plt.ylabel("")
    plt.xlabel("Total Flight Hours")
    first_date, last_date = get_first_date_and_last_date()
    plt.title(
        f"Airlines where we do not have a confident triturator assignment (>1000 flight hours)\n{first_date} - {last_date}"
    )

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()


def start():
    return_destination_trit_plot()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    start()
